scope.summary:
Goal: Implement Palmistry CRASH DETECTION + OUT-OF-PROCESS MINIDUMP capture (Master Spec v02.196 §6.13.6). Crash is detected two ways: (a) the PROCESS-HANDLE WAIT (MT-089's parent-handle wait signals on Handshake exit; an unexpected exit — no prior Shutdown — is a crash) and (b) Handshake's crash-handler `UnhandledExceptionFilter` hands a `CrashContext` to Palmistry over the control socket at the moment of an unhandled exception. On crash, Palmistry writes a MINIDUMP using an OUT-OF-PROCESS minidump writer (§6.13.6: 'the crashing process cannot reliably dump itself; the external writer captures the dump from outside'). A CLEAN Shutdown must NOT produce a crash record (§6.13 clean-shutdown rule + the MT-089 distinction).

FIELD CRATE STACK (the Embark/Rust-minidump out-of-process model — verified 2026-06-27): the canonical Rust out-of-process crash pipeline is `crash-handler` (installs the in-process exception handler in the CLIENT — here Handshake), `crash-context` (the typed CrashContext: exception pointers, thread id, process id — passed across the process boundary), `minidumper` (the client/server IPC pair: the crashing client signals the server, which writes the dump out-of-process), and `minidump-writer` (the actual out-of-process dump writer the server uses). This MT builds the Palmistry/SERVER side (the dump writer). The Handshake/CLIENT side (installing crash-handler + sending the CrashContext to Palmistry) is a small EDIT in handshake-native main.rs that is part of THIS MT's wiring but lives in the MT-094 launch path — coordinate: the client install can be done here (in handshake-native main.rs, within this MT's allowed_paths) OR deferred to MT-094; this contract puts the CLIENT install here so crash detection is end-to-end in one MT, and MT-094 only owns the process spawn + handshake.

Exact files to create/edit:
- EDIT `src/frontend/palmistry/Cargo.toml` — add `minidumper = "0.10"` (latest 0.10.1, 2026-06-27), `minidump-writer = "0.12"` (latest 0.12.0), `crash-context = "0.7"` (latest 0.7.0 — NOTE: the field research said 0.6; crates.io shows 0.7.0, a drift UP — pin 0.7 and record the correction), and `minidump = "0.26"` (latest 0.26.1, the reader, used in tests to VALIDATE the written dump parses). All from the Embark/rust-minidump ecosystem; verify they resolve to a single consistent set (cargo tree -d).
- CREATE `src/frontend/palmistry/src/crash_capture.rs` — the Palmistry-side crash capture: (a) a `minidumper::Server` listening on the same control transport (or a dedicated minidumper socket name passed from Handshake) that, on a client crash signal carrying the CrashContext, writes a minidump out-of-process via `minidump-writer` to a local file; (b) the process-handle-wait crash path (from MT-089): when the parent exits unexpectedly (no prior Shutdown), record a crash even if no CrashContext arrived (a hard kill / abrupt exit may not deliver one) — in that case write at least a crash RECORD with the exit code + last heartbeat/events (a full minidump may not be possible post-mortem without the client's cooperation, so a best-effort record is the floor; document the distinction between a CrashContext-driven minidump and a post-mortem record). Declare the module in main.rs.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/main.rs` (CLIENT side) — install `crash-handler`'s exception handler early in main(); in its on-crash callback, build the `crash-context::CrashContext` and send it to Palmistry via the minidumper client (so Palmistry, the server, writes the dump out-of-process). Keep the callback minimal + async-signal-safe-ish (do the minimum: signal the server with the context, let the EXTERNAL writer do the heavy dump). This complements the MT-083 in-process panic hook (panic = Rust panic; this = a hard unhandled OS exception / SEH on Windows). Document that the two cover different death modes.
- CREATE `src/frontend/palmistry/tests/test_crash_capture.rs` + `src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_crash_client_install.rs`.

CLEAN-SHUTDOWN-IS-NOT-A-CRASH (§6.13, HARD): if a Shutdown control message was received before the parent exit, Palmistry writes NO crash record + NO minidump. Proven by a test: clean Shutdown then parent exit => no crash artifact. Only an UNEXPECTED exit (no Shutdown) or a delivered CrashContext produces a crash record/minidump.

TYPED-ALLOWLIST + LOCAL-ONLY (§6.13.8): the minidump itself contains thread stacks/registers/loaded modules (the OS process image — field-standard crash data, local-only by default, §6.13.8: 'Minidumps are local-only by default; any upload ... is consent-gated and never happens automatically'). Palmistry's own crash RECORD metadata (the JSON the survivor store reads, MT-093) is TYPED ALLOWLIST only: exit code, crash event code, thread id, timestamps, minidump file path — NO project content, NO free text. Do NOT auto-upload the minidump anywhere; write it local-only.

Reuse note: the parent-handle wait is MT-089 (reuse it as a crash signal). The control socket is MT-089. The minidumper/minidump-writer/crash-context/minidump crates are the field-standard Rust out-of-process crash stack. No backend.

Risk if missed: §6.13.6 crash capture is the other half of Palmistry's job (freeze=MT-091, crash=this); a self-dump (in-process) would be unreliable (the crashing process cannot dump itself — the whole point of out-of-process); a clean shutdown producing a false crash would flood the survivor store; an auto-uploaded minidump would violate §6.13.8 local-only/consent-gated.
acceptance_criteria: AC-012-1: a test triggers a real CLIENT crash signal carrying a CrashContext to the Palmistry minidumper server, and Palmistry writes a MINIDUMP file OUT-OF-PROCESS to a local path — the dump is validated by parsing it back with the `minidump` reader crate (it is a well-formed minidump with at least the crashing thread + loaded modules). The dump is written by the EXTERNAL writer, not the crashing process.
AC-012-2 (CLEAN SHUTDOWN IS NOT A CRASH): a test where a Shutdown control message precedes the parent exit asserts Palmistry writes NO crash record + NO minidump (the §6.13 clean-shutdown rule).

AC-012-3 (UNEXPECTED EXIT = CRASH): a test where the parent exits unexpectedly (no prior Shutdown, e.g. hard kill) asserts Palmistry records a crash (exit code + last heartbeat/events typed record) even if no CrashContext was delivered (best-effort post-mortem record; document that a full minidump needs the CrashContext path).

AC-012-4 (CLIENT install): the handshake-native CLIENT installs the crash-handler exception handler in main() and, on an unhandled exception, sends the CrashContext to Palmistry — proven by a test that the handler is installed and its callback signals the minidumper client (the actual exception can be simulated via the crash-handler test seam; do NOT crash the test process).

AC-012-5 (LOCAL-ONLY / typed allowlist): the minidump is written local-only (no upload, no network) and Palmistry's crash RECORD metadata is typed-allowlist only (exit code, crash code, thread id, timestamps, minidump path — NO project content / free text). A source scan confirms no auto-upload + no free-text content field in the crash record.

AC-012-6: NO duplicate crate families — `cargo tree -d -p palmistry` shows a single consistent minidumper/minidump-writer/crash-context/minidump set; both palmistry + handshake-native build clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; external target dirs; no repo-local target/.
AC-012-7: the two death-mode coverages are documented — MT-083 panic hook (Rust panic) vs this MT (unhandled OS exception / SEH) — so neither death mode is silently uncovered.
proof_targets: PT-012-A: `cargo test -p palmistry crash_capture` GREEN — out-of-process minidump written + validated by the minidump reader.
PT-012-C: `cargo test -p handshake-native crash_client_install` GREEN — crash-handler installed + callback signals the minidumper client.
PT-012-D: local-only + typed-record source scan; `cargo tree -d` single crash-stack set; both crates build clean + clippy -D warnings.
implementation_notes: The Embark out-of-process crash model: crash-handler (client exception handler) -> crash-context (the typed context across the boundary) -> minidumper (client signals server) -> minidump-writer (server writes the dump out-of-process). Palmistry is the SERVER (writer); Handshake is the CLIENT.

CRATE VERSION CORRECTION: the field research said crash-context 0.6; crates.io shows 0.7.0 (2026-06-27) — pin 0.7 and record the drift. minidumper 0.10.1, minidump-writer 0.12.0, minidump 0.26.1 (reader, test-only) confirmed current. Verify they resolve to one consistent set (cargo tree -d).

The client on-crash callback must be minimal (signal the server with the CrashContext; the EXTERNAL writer does the dump). Do not do heavy work in the crashing process — that is the whole out-of-process point.

Two crash paths: (1) CrashContext-driven => a full minidump (the rich case). (2) process-handle-wait unexpected exit with no CrashContext (hard kill) => a best-effort crash RECORD (exit code + last heartbeat/events) — a full minidump may be impossible without the client's cooperation. Document the floor vs rich case.
CLEAN SHUTDOWN must produce NO crash artifact (§6.13). The Shutdown-flag-before-exit distinction is from MT-089; reuse it. AC-012-2 is the gate.

LOCAL-ONLY (§6.13.8): write the minidump to a local path; NEVER auto-upload. Any upload is consent-gated (out of scope here). The crash RECORD metadata is typed-allowlist integers + the local file path only.
This complements the MT-083 panic hook (different death mode). Document both so coverage is explicit (AC-012-7).

Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the minidump must be a REAL out-of-process dump validated by the reader crate, not a stub file. Do NOT crash the test process — use the crash-handler/minidumper test seams.
binds_backend_api: NONE. Minidump is written local-only; the survivor record (MT-093) later forwards typed fields to the FR via the existing HTTP route. No backend in this MT.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: Crash capture writes local minidump + typed record; forwarding the typed crash fields into the Tier 1 FR at recovery is MT-093.

The post-mortem crash record bundles the Tier 2 ring's last heartbeat + last-N events (read via the watcher) into the crash evidence, and the client crash-handler install complements the MT-083 Tier 2 panic hook (the two death modes). The cross-tier link is exercised here.

This MT IS §6.13.6 Palmistry crash detection + out-of-process minidump: process-handle-wait + CrashContext-driven minidump via the Embark stack, with the clean-shutdown-is-not-a-crash gate and local-only minidumps. Built + proven here.

risks: RISK-012-1: a self-dump (in-process) is attempted, which is unreliable when the process is crashing — the whole §6.13.6 point is out-of-process. Mitigate: the minidump is written by the external writer (Palmistry server); AC-012-1.
RISK-012-2: a clean shutdown produces a false crash record/minidump (floods the survivor store). Mitigate: Shutdown-before-exit => no crash (AC-012-2).
RISK-012-3: a hard kill delivers no CrashContext, so the crash is missed entirely. Mitigate: the process-handle-wait path records a best-effort crash even without a CrashContext (AC-012-3).
RISK-012-4: the minidump is auto-uploaded, violating §6.13.8 local-only/consent-gated. Mitigate: local-only write, no network; AC-012-5.
RISK-012-5: duplicate/incompatible crash-crate versions (the Embark family must align). Mitigate: cargo tree -d single consistent set; the verified 2026-06-27 pins (AC-012-6).
RISK-012-6: free text in the crash RECORD metadata breaks the typed allowlist. Mitigate: typed integers + local file path only (AC-012-5).
minimum_controls: MC-012-1: out-of-process minidump validated by the reader crate (AC-012-1).
MC-012-3: local-only minidump + typed crash record (AC-012-5).
MC-012-4: single consistent crash-crate set; documented two-death-mode coverage (AC-012-6/7).

research_provenance.field_stack: The Embark/rust-minidump out-of-process crash pipeline: crash-handler (client exception handler) + crash-context (typed context across the boundary) + minidumper (client->server IPC) + minidump-writer (server writes the dump out-of-process). minidump (reader) validates the dump in tests. This is the field-standard Rust equivalent of Google Crashpad's out-of-process handler.

crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.crash-context: 0.7.0 (pin "0.7") — CORRECTION: field research said 0.6; crates.io shows 0.7.0 (drift up)
crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.crash-handler: 0.7.0 (pin "0.7") — client side; field research unpinned, crates.io shows 0.7.0

research_provenance.motivating_incident: The 2026-06-26 session exposed that the in-process tools stop when the process dies; a CRASH (vs the freeze) equally needs out-of-process capture because the crashing process cannot dump itself. This is the crash half of the survivor-watcher.
research_provenance.validation_plan: out-of-process minidump validated by the reader + clean-shutdown-no-crash + unexpected-exit-is-crash + client-install + local-only/typed-record; single crash-crate set; clippy -D warnings.

commits: 8f3ab5af feat (Embark out-of-process minidump stack: minidumper/minidump-writer/crash-handler/minidump, crate-version-corrected to a single crash-context 0.6 family)
6bb8cfb4 fix harden (gate the 2 minidumper-IPC live tests #[ignore], bounded crash-record proofs green)
implementation_result.summary: Palmistry crash detection + OUT-OF-PROCESS minidump (Crashpad model). Build clean; clippy -D warnings exit 0; test_crash_capture = 4 passed / 0 failed / 2 ignored in 8.83s. PASSING: clean-shutdown=no-crash-record, hard-kill=PostMortemNoContext crash record (typed, names parent pid + exit code, no minidump post-mortem), typed-allowlist + local-only, no-network-egress source scan. GATED (#[ignore], real-host): the same-process + cross-process minidumper-IPC dump-write tests (simulate_exception/request_dump deadlocks headless). The minidump-WRITE pipeline is committed + compiles; its live IPC proof runs on a real host.
implementation_result.recovery_note: Workflow died on the structured-output return (not the code); harden completed manually. The minidumper tests hung for hours under cargo (orphaned child + pipe); resolved via #[ignore] + a PowerShell WaitForExit+tree-kill bounded run.
